title: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Domestic Violence Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Domestic Violence Issues 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Domestic Violence Issues Homeless Families Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Human Trafficking Issues Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF COMMUNITY WORKS JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an access point for being placed on Jackson County's Centralized Interest List for housing. 2594 E Barnett Road Suite C Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless, with a focus on serving people experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, or trafficking, as well as youth. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 779-4357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO UNICA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a crisis line for domestic violence and sexual assault. Also provides case management, survivor advocacy, housing help, support groups, parenting support, community education, and help with restraining and stalking orders. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Hispanic and Latino domestic violence and sexual assault survivors Hours: Crisis Line: 24 hours a day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 232-4448 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offer support by providing information and helping with forms related to safety concerns. Assist with crisis planning, restraining orders, crime reporting, mental health counseling, support groups, and cultural advocacy. Provides Safety and Stabilization Assessment (SSA) for domestic violence shelters or housing resources. SSA is required before accessing most direct domestic violence services. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assult Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WORKS JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO UNICA GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT
